question_title: Will the community prediction be higher than 50.00% on 2026-03-26 for the Metaculus question "Will Cloudflare experience another critical incident before May 2026?"?
summary: The CP for the Cloudflare critical incident question is currently at exactly 50.00% (the threshold), with a strong downward trend from 61% to 50% in one week. The status quo resolves NO. Zero critical incidents have occurred since Feb 25 despite multiple significant outages. Two paths to YES exist: a critical incident in the next 10 days (~17%) or CP drifting above 50% without one (~22%). The dominant signal is the downward CP trend combined with rational Bayesian updating suggesting CP should be ~40% by March 26 if no incident occurs. Monte Carlo simulations and direct decomposition both center around 35-38%.

factors.description: CP currently at exactly 50.00% (the threshold) - status quo resolves NO since the question requires strictly above 50%
Strong downward CP trend: 61% to 50% in one week (Mar 7 to Mar 14), suggesting continued decline
Rational Bayesian updating: with 36 days remaining on Mar 26 and CP-implied hazard rate, rational CP should be ~40.5% (well below threshold)
Zero critical incidents on Cloudflare status page since Feb 25 despite multiple outages - none classified as critical in Feb or March 2026 per API data
Cloudflare Code Orange resilience plan implemented after Nov/Dec 2025 incidents may reduce critical incident frequency
Possibility of critical incident occurring before Mar 26 (~15-20% based on historical rate of ~1 per 26-69 days), which would resolve target YES and meta YES
Cloudflare continues to have frequent outages (Feb 16, 18, 20, Mar 3 WAF, multiple March incidents) showing ongoing infrastructure instability
Forecaster stickiness and noise around the 50% Schelling point could push CP back above 50% even without a critical incident

reasoning:
**Final forecast: 0.37 (37% probability of YES)**

The key driver is the strong downward CP trend (61% → 50% in one week) combined with the fact that CP is exactly at the threshold where status quo resolves NO. While a ~17% chance of a critical incident could flip the answer to YES, the more likely path is CP continuing to drift below 50% as rational forecasters update downward with each passing day without a critical incident.

resolution_criteria: This question will resolve based on the community prediction of the Metaculus question found [here](https://www.metaculus.com/questions/42284) (i.e. the 'target question'). If the community prediction of the target question on 2026-03-26 17:49:39 is higher than 50.00%, this question will resolve to 'Yes'. If it is lower or equal to 50.00%, this question will resolve to 'No'. If the target question has already resolved before this question opens, then this question will be annulled. If the target question resolves after this question opens, but before 2026-03-26 17:49:39, then this question will resolve to the same value as the target question.
